meta_title: Impact Doors in Lantana, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Lantana, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home—call today!
h1: Impact Doors for Lantana Homes: Coastal-Grade Protection Built to Last
intro:
Lantana, Florida sits just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic shoreline, and that proximity is part of what makes this community one of Palm Beach County's most charming places to call home. Neighborhoods like Lantana Homes, Pine Ridge, and South Shores offer a laid-back coastal lifestyle within walking distance of Lantana Beach and local staples like the Old Key Lime House. But that same coastline that draws residents in also places every home squarely in the path of serious hurricane-force winds. With a 170 mph wind zone designation and a storm history that includes eight significant named storms since 1990, the question for Lantana homeowners isn't whether to upgrade to impact doors—it's how soon.

Nearly 55% of homes in Lantana were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping changes to the Florida Building Code that followed Hurricane Andrew's devastation. Many of these homes still have standard entry doors, hollow-core French doors, or aging sliding glass doors that offer minimal resistance to windborne debris and structural pressure. In a storm, these openings become the most vulnerable points of your home's envelope. When a door fails during a hurricane, the resulting pressure differential can lift roofs, collapse walls, and cause catastrophic interior damage—often within seconds. For homeowners in Lantana Homes or Pine Ridge whose properties are now valued around $350,000, losing that investment to a preventable failure is simply not an option.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped hundreds of South Florida homeowners close that vulnerability gap with professionally installed, code-compliant impact doors. Whether you're replacing a front entry door, upgrading a set of sliding glass doors overlooking the backyard, or installing elegant French doors that don't compromise on safety, our team brings the right products, the right permits, and the right craftsmanship to every Lantana home we touch. Known throughout Palm Beach County as 'The Kings of Service,' we're here to make sure your doors are the last line of defense you'll ever need to worry about.

section_why.h2: Why Lantana Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home to replace something as critical as your entry or sliding glass doors, experience and accountability matter more than a low quote. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties—and every single installation is performed by our own in-house crews, never subcontracted to third parties. That distinction matters enormously in Lantana, where building conditions vary widely between a 1960s-era block home in Lantana Homes and a newer waterfront property near South Shores.

Our team understands the nuances of working in coastal Palm Beach County—from sourcing the right anchor systems for older concrete block construction to ensuring proper flashing and sealant application in high-humidity coastal environments. We've completed more than 500 homes across the tri-county area, and our process is built around precision: accurate measurements, proper substrate preparation, Town of Lantana Building Department permit coordination, and a final inspection that confirms every door performs to code before we ever leave your property.

Holding an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau isn't something we take lightly. It reflects a commitment to showing up on time, communicating clearly, and standing behind our work long after installation day. We're fully licensed and insured, and we carry the documentation to prove it. For Lantana homeowners comparing bids, that accountability is worth every penny. Fly-by-night operators with no local footprint can't offer what Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can: a team you can call next hurricane season, next year, or five years from now, and get an actual answer.

We also recognize that no two Lantana homes are identical. Our consultants take the time to assess your specific door openings, your home's construction type, your aesthetic preferences, and your budget before recommending any product. Whether you're focused purely on storm protection or you also want to enhance your home's curb appeal along one of Lantana's tree-lined streets, we'll find the solution that checks every box.

section_history.h2: Lantana's Hurricane History Makes a Strong Case for Impact Door Upgrades
section_history.body:
Lantana has been tested repeatedly by the Atlantic hurricane machine. Hurricane Frances made landfall along the Treasure Coast in September 2004 as a Category 2 storm, sending sustained tropical-storm-force winds across Palm Beach County for hours and causing widespread structural damage to older door and window systems throughout the area. Just thirteen months later, Hurricane Wilma—a storm that holds the record for the most intense Atlantic hurricane ever recorded—tore across South Florida in October 2005, bringing Category 3 winds to Palm Beach County and peeling away anything not properly anchored. Residents near Sportsman's Park and along the coastal corridors of Lantana saw firsthand how quickly standard doors buckle under that kind of sustained pressure and debris impact.

Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017. Though it weakened somewhat before making its South Florida landfall, Irma's sprawling wind field still subjected Lantana and neighboring communities like Lake Worth Beach and Hypoluxo to hours of tropical storm and Category 1-equivalent gusts. Insurance claims in Palm Beach County following Irma numbered in the tens of thousands, with door and window failures accounting for a significant portion of interior water damage claims. Many homeowners discovered too late that their doors—particularly older sliding glass units and decorative French doors—simply weren't up to the task.

With eight named storms having impacted this area since 1990, and climate projections suggesting continued active Atlantic hurricane seasons ahead, the pattern is clear. Lantana's coastal position, just 1.5 miles from the ocean, means these storms arrive with little dissipation from inland friction. The right impact doors don't just protect your home during a storm—they give you and your family confidence that your largest investment is sealed tight, no matter what the season brings.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Lantana and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Lantana is located in Palm Beach County and, while it falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that covers Miami-Dade and Broward counties, it is absolutely not exempt from rigorous hurricane protection requirements. Under the current Florida Building Code, all exterior door replacements in Lantana must meet wind-load resistance standards appropriate for the area's 170 mph design wind speed. This means any impact door installed on a Lantana home must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or product approval demonstrating compliance with ASTM E1886 and E1996 impact and pressure testing standards—the same core standards used in HVHZ, though with category-appropriate thresholds for Palm Beach County's wind zone.

All impact door replacements in Lantana require a permit issued through the Town of Lantana Building Department. This includes entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French door systems. Permit requirements typically include submission of product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing credentials. After installation, a final inspection by the Town's building official is required to verify that the installation meets code. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire permitting process on behalf of our clients—we handle the paperwork, coordinate inspection scheduling, and make sure every installation closes with a passed inspection on record.

For homes built before 1994, there are additional considerations. Older door frames and rough openings may require reinforcement or modification to properly support a modern impact door system. Our installation team evaluates each opening during the pre-installation assessment to identify any structural work needed before the new door goes in. Skipping this step—as some discount installers do—can result in a product approval that looks correct on paper but fails in practice because the surrounding structure wasn't properly prepared. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we do it right from the first measurement to the final inspection stamp.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: The Right Brands for Lantana's Coastal Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two trusted manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned strong reputations across South Florida for producing impact-rated door systems that combine structural integrity with refined aesthetics—a combination that matters to Lantana homeowners who want protection without sacrificing the look and feel of their homes.

ESW's impact door line includes heavy-duty entry door systems, impact-rated sliding glass doors, and French door configurations, all engineered to withstand the wind pressures and debris impacts associated with a 170 mph wind zone. ESW doors feature multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame rather than a single latch point, distributing load across the entire door during a storm and dramatically reducing the risk of forced entry or wind failure. Their laminated glass inserts use the same impact-resistant technology found in automotive windshields—designed to crack under extreme impact but remain bonded to an interlayer rather than shattering into dangerous shards.

CWI products complement the ESW lineup with additional options in sliding glass door configurations and specialty door systems suited for Lantana's diverse housing stock. CWI's impact sliding doors are a popular choice for homes in South Shores and Pine Ridge where rear-facing lanais and pool decks call for large glass openings that still need to meet code. Both ESW and CWI products are Florida Product Approved, meaning they've been independently tested and verified to meet the state's stringent standards. For Lantana homeowners investing in long-term storm protection for a home valued around $350,000, these are brands you can trust to still be performing perfectly a decade from now.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Lantana—and How Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Makes Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in Lantana varies depending on the type of door—entry, sliding glass, or French—as well as the size of the opening, the specific product selected, and any structural modifications needed to the existing rough opening. For a single impact entry door, homeowners in Lantana typically invest between $1,500 and $3,500 installed. Impact sliding glass doors generally range from $2,500 to $6,000 depending on width and panel configuration, while French door systems with sidelights can run from $3,000 to $7,000 or more. For homes with multiple door openings—a common scenario in Lantana's older ranch-style homes—bundling the project often results in better per-unit pricing.

For a home valued at $350,000, impact door upgrades represent a relatively small percentage of the home's value while delivering outsized protection and return on investment. Studies consistently show that impact-rated openings increase resale value, reduce buyer hesitation in South Florida's competitive real estate market, and qualify homeowners for meaningful insurance premium discounts—often enough to offset a significant portion of the installation cost over time.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified buyers, making it possible for middle-income Lantana homeowners to move forward with protection upgrades without depleting savings or waiting for the next storm to force the issue. Our financing is straightforward, with no hidden fees and terms designed to fit real household budgets. When you factor in insurance savings, increased home value, and the avoided cost of storm damage repair, impact doors in Lantana are one of the most financially sound home improvement decisions you can make.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Lantana Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate financial benefits of installing impact doors in Lantana is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts for homes with wind-mitigation features, and impact-rated openings—including doors—are among the most heavily weighted factors in wind mitigation assessments. After installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing clients typically schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector, who documents the impact-rated products and their compliance. That inspection report is then submitted to your insurance carrier to trigger the applicable premium discounts.

In Palm Beach County, where insurance premiums have risen sharply in recent years due to the state's difficult insurance market, wind mitigation credits can be substantial. Many Lantana homeowners report annual savings ranging from $500 to over $1,500 per year depending on their carrier, coverage level, and the scope of their impact upgrades. Major carriers operating in this market—including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance—all recognize wind mitigation improvements and adjust premiums accordingly. Over a ten-year horizon, those savings can total $5,000 to $15,000, turning your impact door investment into a net financial gain.

Beyond the premium discount, impact doors also protect you from the devastating out-of-pocket costs that follow a storm-related interior loss. When a standard door fails during a hurricane, the resulting water intrusion and structural damage can easily exceed $20,000 to $50,000 in repairs—and insurance deductibles for hurricane claims in Florida are typically calculated as a percentage of the home's insured value, not a flat dollar amount. For a $350,000 home with a 2% hurricane deductible, that means $7,000 comes out of your pocket before coverage kicks in. Impact doors significantly reduce the likelihood of that scenario ever occurring.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if I'm not directly on the beach in Lantana?
faqs.a: Yes. Lantana is just 1.5 miles from the coast, and that proximity means your home is fully exposed to coastal hurricane winds without meaningful inland wind reduction. The Town of Lantana sits in a 170 mph wind zone, and homes throughout every neighborhood—including Pine Ridge and Lantana Homes—are subject to the same wind-load requirements. Impact doors aren't just for oceanfront properties; they're essential protection for any home in this area.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Lantana, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Lantana typically ranges from $1,500 to $7,000 per opening depending on door type, size, and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified buyers, so you can protect your home without a large upfront payment. We'll walk you through all financing options during your free in-home consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Lantana, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact door replacements in Lantana require a permit from the Town of Lantana Building Department, along with a final inspection after installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process for you—we prepare the documentation, submit the application, coordinate with the building department, and schedule the required inspection. You don't have to navigate that process alone.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get impact doors installed in Lantana?
faqs.a: The typical timeline from signed contract to completed installation is 4 to 8 weeks, depending on product lead times and permit processing speed at the Town of Lantana Building Department. Our team will provide you with a realistic schedule during the consultation and keep you updated throughout the process. Most single-door installations are completed in one day once the permit is issued and the product arrives.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Lantana?
faqs.a: Very likely, yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts for homes with wind-mitigation features, and impact-rated doors are a qualifying upgrade. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your new doors and provide a report for your carrier. Lantana homeowners commonly see annual premium reductions of $500 to $1,500 or more, depending on their insurer and overall home protection level. Over time, these savings often offset a significant portion of the installation cost.
